summary.intent: Investigate and implement CHA-9 (P1.2: Define CRM types) — enrich the existing Contact struct and add Matter, Interaction, ContactChannel structs plus ContactStage, MatterStatus, MatterPhase, PracticeArea enums in the Rust CRM crate.
summary.outcome: Investigation summary posted to Linear, full implementation committed on a feature branch with 53/53 tests passing, clippy clean, and rustfmt applied. All 8 required types defined across types.rs, store.rs, store_sqlite.rs, store_memory.rs, error.rs, and a new migration.

repo: secrecy v0.8's Serialize impl for Secret<T> is intentionally opt-in via the SerializableSecret marker trait — deriving Serialize on a struct containing Secret<String> will not compile without custom serde helpers.
Custom serde helpers for Secret<String> are already established in crates/oauth/src/types.rs and should be replicated (not imported) into other crates that store PII in SQLite.
All enums follow the ChannelType pattern: derive Debug/Clone/Copy/PartialEq/Eq/Serialize/Deserialize + serde(rename_all = 'snake_case') + as_str() + Display + FromStr impls.
Timestamps are u64 millis throughout the codebase — no chrono or SystemTime types at the domain layer.
secrecy = { workspace = true } is available as a workspace dependency (v0.8 with serde feature) and is used extensively in oauth, auth, and providers crates.
The existing Contact struct had source/external_id fields that were kept for backward compat while ContactChannel provides multi-channel identity.

code.finding: Contains the canonical serde helper pattern for Secret<String> round-trip serialization — reference implementation for any crate that needs PII field serde.

opt_secret_eq helper and custom serialize/deserialize helpers for Secret<String> fields were added here; ExposeSecret must be imported at the module level to avoid duplicate imports inside nested scopes.
Migration naming convention is timestamp-prefixed; new migration must sort after 20260317000000_init.sql which only covered the contacts table.

workflow: The LINEAR_API_KEY env var is available in the bash shell but is not forwarded to ctx_execute subprocesses — a temp-file workaround was needed to pass the key to MCP tool calls.
Running clippy and rustfmt as separate quality gates after tests is necessary; rustfmt produced diffs that required a separate apply step before the commit.
The autonomous agent workflow uses a Linear comment as a persistent workpad (## Workpad) that is appended to across runs, never overwritten — this preserves decision history across rework iterations.

friction: LINEAR_API_KEY not propagated to ctx_execute subprocess environment — required writing key to a temp file and reading it inside the tool call, adding several extra steps.
secrecy v0.8 Serialize compile error was not anticipated despite being documented in the crate — caused multiple edits to types.rs to retrofit custom serde helpers after the initial compile failure.
rustfmt produced formatting differences after implementation was complete, requiring an additional apply-and-recommit pass.
open_items: PracticeArea extensibility: currently a closed enum — may need to support custom/user-defined values for non-legal CRM use cases.
CrmStore trait splitting: single trait will eventually violate ISP as matter/interaction CRUD methods are added — splitting into MatterStore/InteractionStore traits deferred.
Secret<String> serde helpers duplicated from crates/oauth/src/types.rs into crates/crm/src/types.rs — a shared internal crate for PII serde utilities would eliminate the duplication.
store_sqlite.rs SQLite row types and queries for the new tables (matters, interactions, contact_channels) were written but not explicitly verified in the transcript against the new migration schema.
